versions.version_description: The tab is on 2nd fret while other versions are with no capo.

tuning: E A D G B E
content:
SONG: Born in the USA
ARTIST: Bruce Springsteen
ALBUM: Born in the USA
TABBED BY: Kev Short

Start out with clean guitar then put a bit of distortion on when the song kicks in fully.
For the faster parts of the song, strumming the chords instead of simply playing them and 
letting them ring could sound better.


[tab]For the keyboard melody play this:
  ([ch]B[/ch])
e|-------------------------|-4-----------------------|
B|-------7-----------5--7--|----5-----7--------------|
G|-------------------------|-------------------------| x2
D|-------------------------|-------------------------|
A|-------------------------|-------------------------|
E|-7-----------------------|-------------------------|[/tab]

[tab]  ([ch]E[/ch])
e|-------------------------|-4-----------------------|
B|-------7-----------5--7--|----5-----7--------------|
G|-------------------------|-------------------------| x2
D|-------------------------|-------------------------|
A|-------------------------|-------------------------|
E|-0-----------------------|-------------------------|[/tab]


[Intro]

[ch]B[/ch]   [ch]B[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]


[Verse 1]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]
  Born down in a dead man's town[/tab]

The first kick I took was when I hit the ground
[tab][ch]E[/ch]
End up like a dog that's been beat too much[/tab]

Till you spend half your life just covering up, now


[Chorus]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]
  Born in the U.S.A., I was born in the U.S.A.[/tab]
[tab]      [ch]E[/ch]
I was   born in the U.S.A., born in the U.S.A. now[/tab]


[Verse 2]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]
  Got in a little hometown jam[/tab]

So they put a rifle in my hand
[tab][ch]E[/ch]
  Sent me off to a foreign land[/tab]

To go and kill the yellow man


[Chorus]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]
  Born in the U.S.A., I was born in the U.S.A.[/tab]
[tab]      [ch]E[/ch]
I was   born in the U.S.A., I was born in the U.S.A. [/tab]


[Verse 3]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]
  Come back home to the refinery[/tab]

Hiring man said "Son if it was up to me"
[tab][ch]E[/ch]
  Went down to see my V.A. man[/tab]

He said "Son, don't you understand now"


[Instrumental]

[ch]B[/ch]   [ch]B[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch] 


[Verse 4]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]
  I had a brother at Khe Sahn[/tab]

Fighting off the Viet Cong
[tab][ch]E[/ch]
  They're still there, he's all gone[/tab]
[ch]E[/ch]


[Verse 5]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]
  He had a woman he loved in Saigon[/tab]

I got a picture of him in her arms now

[ch]E[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]


[Verse 6]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]
  Down in the shadow of the penitentiary[/tab]

Out by the gas fires of the refinery
[tab]    [ch]E[/ch]
I'm   ten years burning down the road[/tab]

Nowhere to run ain't got nowhere to go


[Chorus]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]
  Born in the U.S.A., I was born in the U.S.A. now[/tab]
[tab][ch]E[/ch]
  Born in the U.S.A., I'm a long gone Daddy in the U.S.A. now[/tab]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]
  Born in the U.S.A., Born in the U.S.A.[/tab]
[tab][ch]E[/ch]
  Born in the U.S.A., I'm a cool rocking Daddy in the U.S.A. now[/tab]


[Instrumental]

[ch]B[/ch]   [ch]B[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]   x3


[False Ending]

[ch]B[/ch]   [ch]B[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]

1 2 3 4

(continue)
[ch]B[/ch]   [ch]B[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]
[tab][ch]B[/ch]   [ch]B[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]
(fade out)[/tab]
